channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Paxton bests Cornyn in Texas Republican Senate primary after Trump endorsement
item.description: President Donald Trump last week endorsed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton over incumbent Sen. John Cornyn.

item.title: SK Hynix hits $1 trillion valuation as AI boom lifts South Korean chip stocks
item.description: Shares of SK Hynix soared over 11% on Wednesday, pushing the South Korean memory-chip maker above the $1 trillion market capitalization mark.

item.title: Russia to allow its central bank to down drones as Moscow struggles to defend against attacks
item.description: Russia's State Duma has passed a law allowing certain financial institutions to operate anti-drone defense systems after attacks on its territory.

item.title: Stock futures are little changed after the S&P 500 closes at another record: Live updates
item.description: The S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite rose to fresh intraday and closing records on Tuesday.

item.title: SK Hynix has surged 250% this year. Analysts say the AI-fueled rally may be only halfway done
item.description: Analysts say the AI memory cycle still has years to run, with limited new capacity and surging cloud investment supporting further upside for SK Hynix.

item.title: SpaceX-Tesla merger chatter reignites as Musk pushes rocket company towards Nasdaq
item.description: With SpaceX headed for the public markets next month, industry experts and people close to Elon Musk are speculating about a potential tie-up with Tesla.

item.title: CNBC Daily Open: The $1 trillion club just got bigger
item.description: South Korea's SK Hynix and U.S. chip firm Micron become the latest companies to top $1 trillion in market capitalization as the AI rally resumes.

item.title: Federal judge had sex in chambers with high-ranking police officer, panel says
item.description: The judge sits in a district court in either Alabama, Georgia or Florida, but their name is being kept secret by a disciplinary panel.

item.title: Dulux maker's stock soars 15% after takeover bid from major rival; oil holds below $100
item.description: European stocks were in positive territory on Wednesday as regional investors assess the latest military operations against Iran.

item.title: ECB 'will do what is necessary' to tame inflation, Bank of France governor tells CNBC
item.description: Markets are overwhelmingly pricing in a rate hike from the central bank at its next meeting.

item.title: Barriers grow for international students seeking U.S. jobs: The 'American dream ... is collapsing'
item.description: International graduates say a weak hiring market and changing immigration rules make it harder for them to achieve their American dream of working in the U.S.

item.title: Americans are 'entrenched' in financial stress amid debt and price pressures
item.description: Inflation and high debt levels continue cause financial anxiety for many Americans. Nonprofits can help with debt mangement.

item.title: European companies double down on China manufacturing despite EU de-risking push
item.description: Low manufacturing costs in China are keeping many European businesses' supply chains in the country despite pressure in the EU to reduce overseas reliance.

item.title: Markets suggest stocks are on solid ground and crude oil could be in for a decline, Todd Gordon says
item.description: The VIX is saying that equities are in good shape and, if a resolution of the war is near, crude oil may plummet into the $80s, and maybe $70s, per barrel.

item.title: Why the UAE walked away from OPEC
item.description: The United Arab Emirates’ decision to leave OPEC after nearly 60 years marks a major shift in the global oil order. Officially framed as an economic decision, the move reflects Abu Dhabi’s frustration with production limits, its push to monetize oil reserves and a changing energy market where non-OPEC+ producers are gaining influence. But the timing also points to deeper geopolitical forces: the Iran war, shifting Gulf alliances and Abu Dhabi’s closer alignment with the U.S.

item.title: China industrial profits jump 24.7% in April, fastest gain in over two years despite headwinds
item.description: China’s industrial profits grew at their fastest pace in over two years in April, buoyed by stronger exports, higher producer prices and gains in upstream industries.

item.title: JPMorgan says it's time to buy these unloved safe stocks that pay dividends
item.description: The bank sees an attractive entry point for low-volatility stocks. These are the companies JPMorgan rates overweight.

item.title: Chip stocks continue to surge. Here's how to buy into the trend for less
item.description: Mike Khouw happens to like one surging chip stock in particular: Marvell Technology.

item.title: Asia-Pacific markets trade mixed as investors weigh Iran tensions and ceasefire talks
item.description: Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on Wednesday as investors assessed recent U.S. military action in Iran.

item.title: Apple's surge to record highs faces a major test next month. What it must do to pass
item.description: The iPhone maker's WWDC kicks off on June 8, offering Apple another chance to impress Wall Street on AI.

item.title: Piper Sandler says Strait of Hormuz to remain closed for months and oil to hit new highs
item.description: Prolonged closure of Strait of Hormuz could send crude oil prices to new highs this summer.

item.title: Your credit card could help cut the cost of your summer vacation
item.description: CNBC Select goes over some of the most overlooked credit card benefits that could help you save money on your summer travels.

item.title: Trump officials split over Pope Leo’s AI warning as Vatican feud enters new front
item.description: Pope Leo XIV’s AI warning shows split among Trump officials as the White House resists new guardrails, upping stakes with Catholic voters and Silicon Valley allies.

item.title: Trump cancels Camp David Cabinet trip, citing weather
item.description: Trump has rarely visited Camp David during either of his presidential terms, though he frequently spends time outside the White House at his own properties.

item.title: A battle is brewing in the gold pits. Here are the winners and losers
item.description: Options volumes leaned bullish in both the SPDR Gold ETF (GLD) and VanEck Gold Miners ETF (GDX) on Tuesday.

item.title: American Airlines picks SpaceX's Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi on more than 500 planes
item.description: American Airlines said it will install SpaceX's Starlink for inflight Wi-Fi on more than 500 of its airplanes.

item.title: U.S. conducts 'self-defense strikes' in Iran as Trump pushes for peace deal
item.description: Targets included missile launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to emplace mines.

item.title: AutoZone stock has worst day in four years, despite retailer beating Wall Street estimates
item.description: Concerns include international growth and margin compression as well as pressures from inflation, energy costs and potential supply chain disruptions.

item.title: Democratic attorneys general snub Vance's anti-fraud roundtable at White House after late invite
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is leading an initiative by the Trump administration to reduce fraud in federally funded programs that are administered by states.

item.title: Volvo Cars wins U.S. approval to keep importing vehicles with 'connected car' technology
item.description: Volvo Cars said Tuesday it had received approval from the U.S. allowing it to continue selling vehicles with Chinese "connected technology" in the country.
